summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the new SEO Blog feature on ViceHub. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases pass (auth login + 401, /api/auth/me with/without token, all admin-only blog endpoints require Bearer, full create/get/update/delete CRUD with slug auto-gen + duplicate -2 suffix verified, image upload + public serve, sitemap.xml contains /, /blog and per-post URLs on public domain). Frontend admin: /admin redirects to /admin/login when logged out; wrong password shows 'Invalid email or password' in [data-testid=admin-login-error]; correct login lands on /admin dashboard; New Post -> editor -> save creates post (appears in admin-posts-list); edit-post-{slug} loads existing content; preview-tab renders markdown (bold verified); update saves; delete-post-{slug} removes it from both admin list and public /blog. Frontend public: /blog grid lists card, clicking blog-card-{slug} opens /blog/{slug} with [data-testid=blog-post] rendering markdown content (post-content has ), and SEO is complete: document.title='E2E Blog ... — ViceHub Journal', og:title, og:image, og:type=article, twitter:title, canonical=https://.../blog/{slug}, and script#ld-json-article containing valid Article JSON-LD ALL present. 0 console errors on /blog and /blog/:slug. CRITICAL BUG FOUND: clicking the new 'Blog' link in Navbar (nav-link-blog and mobile-nav-link-blog) on landing '/' throws 'ReferenceError: navigate is not defined' and displays a full-screen React error overlay — useNavigate was never imported in /app/frontend/src/components/Navbar.jsx (used on lines 55 and 93).

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking nav-link-blog or mobile-nav-link-blog throws 'ReferenceError: navigate is not defined' — useNavigate from 'react-router-dom' is never imported / instantiated. The onClick handlers on lines 55 and 93 call navigate('/blog') with no such binding in scope. In dev/preview this shows the React Uncaught runtime errors overlay covering the whole landing page; users cannot navigate to /blog from the navbar.

integration_issues.issue: Regression item 'nav Blog link navigates to /blog' fails because of the Navbar bug above. Direct URL /blog works correctly.

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/components/Navbar.jsx: add `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';` at the top and `const navigate = useNavigate();` inside the Navbar component. This removes the runtime crash and restores Blog nav-link routing on both desktop and mobile.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx uses navigate('/blog') without importing useNavigate — root cause of the landing crash. Fix: add useNavigate hook.

On a post page where excerpt is empty, meta description falls back to the site-level description rather than a post-specific one. Consider deriving description from a truncated content snippet when excerpt is empty so per-post SEO descriptions are always meaningful.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') hooks; migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers (non-blocking).

Auth uses Bearer token in localStorage (vh_admin_token) per spec — not httpOnly cookies. CORS allow_credentials=True is currently combined with allow_origins='*' from env; if cookies are ever introduced, origins must become an explicit list (browser blocks credentials+wildcard).

Slug regeneration on PUT uses body.slug || body.title; PostEditor sends explicit slug so titles changed via the editor keep the original slug (observed in test — 'E2E Blog ...v2' kept slug 'e2e-blog-...'). Verify this is intentional (it is the safer default for SEO/permalinks).

success_rate.frontend: 92% (1 critical Navbar bug; all admin + public blog + SEO flows pass)

test_credentials: admin@vicehub.gg / ViceHub@2026 (Bearer token via POST /api/auth/login, stored in localStorage 'vh_admin_token').
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted one blog post during testing ('E2E Blog 1782477945' -> 'E2E Blog 1782477945 v2'); pytest CRUD test creates two TEST_-titled posts and deletes them. No residual data in Supabase from this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suites: /app/backend/tests/test_vicehub_backend.py (waitlist + companion SSE) and /app/backend/tests/test_vicehub_blog.py (auth + blog CRUD + image upload + sitemap). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. The blog suite uses ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_PASSWORD env (defaults match seed). For frontend, after the Navbar fix, retest nav-link-blog and mobile-nav-link-blog click -> /blog navigation, then verify no 'navigate is not defined' overlay appears anywhere. Use unique titles when creating posts (timestamp suffix). PostEditor preserves the original slug on title edits — use the *original* slug for delete-post-{slug} testid.
rca of the issue: Navbar.jsx (commit adding the Blog link) introduced two onClick handlers calling navigate(...) but the file does not import useNavigate from react-router-dom nor declare a navigate variable. React renders the component fine, but the click throws ReferenceError, which React 18's runtime error overlay surfaces as a full-screen dev-time overlay on the landing page. Reproduction: load '/', click 'Blog' in the desktop nav (or open the mobile menu and click 'Blog'). Mitigation: add `import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom';` and `const navigate = useNavigate();` inside the Navbar functional component, OR replace the buttons with <Link to="/blog">.
